title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Deployment Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective least-exposure container network topology; isolate the first causal fault in bridge networks, dns, ports, routing, ipv6, and exposure policy and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-exposure container network topology and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress. Competency probe: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.

Inspect the Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, nat, firewall rules, and platform boundaries. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge, keep Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics-1ul5pf". Tailor its network-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, keep Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics-1ul5pf
  - competency: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics
    artifact: network-contract.yaml
    decision: ipv6 host platform diagnostics
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: recovery host starting from an empty project namespace
    fault-injection: fill the writable layer before graceful termination
    retained-proof: shutdown duration exit state and durable-work reconciliation
    case-key: 1ul5pf
    change: |
      frontend-network: published-edge
      backend-network: internal
      service-discovery: service-name
      host-binding: 127.0.0.1:8080
      database-published: false
    verify: docker network inspect intake-backend --format json
    failure: Reject container IP pinning, localhost-as-peer assumptions, accidental all-interface publishing, or an exposed database port.
    repair: Use project-scoped DNS names, separate networks by trust, bind only the required host interface, and test egress and ingress independently.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A debug Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress. Competency probe: Diagnose IPv4, IPv6, Desktop VM, host access, DNS, proxy, firewall, MTU, and published-path differences with layered evidence.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: One successful ping proves DNS, TCP, TLS, application readiness, and published-port policy.
